entries.name: FeeBank
entries.description: Custody contract for resolver fee-token deposits. Depositing increases the resolver's credit in the associated charger contract, while withdrawing reduces credit and returns only the still-available amount. The owner can collect credit already consumed as protocol fees and can rescue arbitrary assets held by the contract.

constructorArgs.description: Deployment configuration: credit charger, fee token and initial owner.

charger.description: Contract that accounts for available resolver credit and is called on every deposit and withdrawal.

feeToken.description: Token held as resolver credit and paid out on withdrawals or fee collection.

owner.description: Owner allowed to collect consumed fees and rescue assets from the FeeBank.

receivedPermissions.description: choose the authorized resolver set by minting, burning or force-transferring RES tokens, authorize token mints or transfers by signature, and transfer ownership.

entries.name: EscrowDst
entries.description: Implementation for deterministic destination-chain escrow clones. Each clone holds the taker's destination token amount plus a native-token safety deposit. During the private withdrawal window only the taker can reveal the secret and release the tokens to the maker; during the public window an access-token holder can perform the same action. After cancellation starts, only the taker can recover the destination tokens. The caller executing a withdrawal or cancellation receives the safety deposit. After 8d, the taker can rescue any remaining token or native balance from the clone.

FACTORY.description: Factory that deployed this implementation. Clone calls validate all supplied swap parameters by recomputing the deterministic clone address against this factory.

RESCUE_DELAY.description: Delay after clone deployment before the swap's taker can rescue arbitrary remaining balances.

PROXY_BYTECODE_HASH.description: Hash of the minimal-proxy creation code used with the swap parameters to validate a clone's deterministic address.

accessToken.description: Immutable token checked for the public withdrawal path. This value is taken from the implementation's internal deployment because it has no public getter.

receivedPermissions.description: collect fee-token credit consumed from resolver accounts, rescue arbitrary ERC20 or native-token balances (including funds backing unused resolver credit), and transfer ownership.

entries.name: EscrowFactory
entries.description: Fusion+ factory and 1inch Limit Order Protocol extension. A source-chain order fill calls this contract through the configured Limit Order Protocol, validates the resolver against the order's time-dependent whitelist or access token, optionally charges the resolver fee, and deploys a deterministic EscrowSrc clone holding the maker's tokens. On the destination chain, anyone can fund and create the matching EscrowDst clone. Partial fills use Merkle proofs so each cumulative fill is bound to a different secret hash.

constructorArgs.description: Deployment configuration: Limit Order Protocol, fee token, resolver access token, initial FeeBank owner, and source/destination rescue delays.

limitOrderProtocol.description: 1inch Limit Order Protocol allowed to call the pre-interaction, post-interaction and partial-fill proof hooks.

feeToken.description: Token deposited in the FeeBank and charged to eligible resolvers when an order enables a resolver fee.

accessToken.description: Token whose holders can fill an order after its configured allowed time when they are not in its time-dependent whitelist. The same token gates the public withdrawal and cancellation functions on escrow clones.

ESCROW_SRC_IMPLEMENTATION.description: Implementation used by deterministic minimal-proxy escrows on the source chain. The implementation itself does not hold per-swap funds.

ESCROW_DST_IMPLEMENTATION.description: Implementation used by deterministic minimal-proxy escrows on the destination chain. The implementation itself does not hold per-swap funds.

FEE_BANK.description: FeeBank deployed with the factory. It holds resolver fee-token deposits and forwards credit changes to this factory.

lastValidated.description: For a partially fillable order, stores the most recently proven secret index and hash. This prevents reuse or out-of-order use of secrets from the order's Merkle tree.

entries.name: ResolverAccessToken
entries.description: Resolver Access Token (RES) used by this Fusion+ deployment. A balance of one marks an address as an authorized resolver: after an order's configured allowed time the holder can fill it outside its time-dependent whitelist (paying a fee when enabled), and after the relevant escrow timelocks the holder can execute public withdrawals or source-chain cancellations to the predetermined recipient in exchange for the safety deposit.

constructorArgs.description: Deployment configuration: token name, symbol and initial owner.

owner.description: Owner controlling token issuance, revocation and transfers.

tokenOwners.description: Latest owner of every token ID, reconstructed from Transfer events. Burned token IDs map to the zero address.

entries.name: EscrowSrc
entries.description: Implementation for deterministic source-chain escrow clones. Each clone holds the maker's source token amount plus a resolver-funded native-token safety deposit. During the private withdrawal window only the swap's taker can reveal the secret and withdraw the tokens (optionally to another address); during the public window an access-token holder can do so only to the taker. After cancellation starts, the taker—or later an access-token holder—can return the tokens to the maker. The caller executing a withdrawal or cancellation receives the safety deposit. After 8d, the taker can rescue any remaining token or native balance from the clone.

FACTORY.description: Factory that deployed this implementation. Clone calls validate all supplied swap parameters by recomputing the deterministic clone address against this factory.

RESCUE_DELAY.description: Delay after clone deployment before the swap's taker can rescue arbitrary remaining balances.

PROXY_BYTECODE_HASH.description: Hash of the minimal-proxy creation code used with the swap parameters to validate a clone's deterministic address.

accessToken.description: Immutable token checked for the public withdrawal and public cancellation paths. This value is taken from the implementation's internal deployment because it has no public getter.
